behavior_summary: Provides event handlers for an hourglass item: an animation tick handler that advances/toggles visual frames and plays a sound on certain transitions, a look handler that describes the object, and a use handler that reports the current in-game time/phase to the player.

HOURGLAS::anim (the main process) appears to spawn a small timing helper (FREE::1C19), suspend to wait for ticks, then on each resume read the item's animation frame and toggle it between paired frames (0<->1 and 2<->3). Transitions from the higher frame back to the lower frame (1->0 and 3->2) trigger a sound effect.
HOURGLAS::look simply causes the item to 'bark' the short description "clock " to the caller/process.

HOURGLAS::use queries a global time service (FREE::28F9) to obtain a numeric time/period code (0..5), maps that code to a human-readable period name (e.g., "Bloodwatch.", "FirstEbb.", "DayTide.", "ThreeMoons.", "LastEbb.", "EvenTide."), constructs the message "The time is now <Name>" and barks it; if no valid code is found it falls back to "This clock is broken ".
notable_control_flow: U8P_HOURGLAS::HOURGLAS::anim runs as a process: it spawns FREE::1C19 at start, uses suspend/resume semantics to create periodic behavior, and uses a small saved temp slot around implies/suspend sequences.
Frame-reading and setting are conditional: for frames 0/1 and 2/3 the handler toggles to the paired frame; when toggling from the higher to the lower frame it also invokes playSFX.
The look and use handlers follow the same suspend/imply flow pattern for short immediate replies (prepare, call Item::bark, resume/return).
open_questions: Exact timing/frequency and implementation details of the spawned FREE::1C19 helper: it is inferred to be a timer/tick driver but its parameters and scheduling semantics are not shown directly.
Whether other engine events (fast-area enter/leave) can trigger these handlers in addition to the explicit 'U8P_HOURGLAS::anim', 'look', and 'use' events observed in the disassembly.
If the hourglass shares frames or sounds with other items (frame indices and sound id usage are numeric and not named here), so asset mapping is not fully resolved.
Whether the 'broken' fallback in use() can occur in normal gameplay or only under unusual circumstances (e.g., time service unavailable).

main_function.name: U8P_HOURGLAS::anim
main_function.purpose: Drive the hourglass item's periodic animation: spawn a timing helper, suspend and resume on ticks, toggle the item's animation frame between paired indices (0<->1 and 2<->3) and play a sound effect on each transition from the higher frame back to the lower frame.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as the item's animation tick handler (Event 2 / 'U8P_HOURGLAS::anim'), likely started via a spawn so it runs as a process tied to the item.
Activated when the animation needs advancing (inferred from spawn/suspend/rotate flow).

function_map.name: U8P_HOURGLAS::anim
function_map.role: Periodic animation/process handler for the hourglass item; toggles frames and triggers SFX on certain transitions.

function_map.name: U8P_HOURGLAS::look
function_map.role: Responds to 'look' events by barking the short description string for the object to the caller.

function_map.name: U8P_HOURGLAS::use
function_map.role: Responds to 'use' events: queries a global time service to format and bark a message describing the current time/period or a broken-clock fallback.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Reads the current animation frame index of an item instance.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Sets the animation frame index of an item instance.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Causes an item to emit/display a text message to a player/process (used by look and use).

function_map.name: U8P_HOURGLAS::playSFX
function_map.role: Plays a sound effect with numeric ids/params; used on specific frame transitions.

function_map.name: FREE::28F9
function_map.role: Global/time service queried by use() to obtain a numeric time/phase code (0..5) used to select a human-readable period name.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper (inferred timing helper) used to drive U8P_HOURGLAS::anim()'s periodic resume/tick behavior.

internal_calls.purpose: Does not call other internal U8P_HOURGLAS handlers; acts as an independent process-driven routine.
Self-contained helper that does not invoke other internal handlers.
Self-contained helper that queries externals but does not call other internal handlers.

external_calls.purpose: Read the current frame index to decide toggle behavior.
Set the new animation frame after toggling.
Play a sound effect when the animation transitions from the upper frame to the lower frame (1->0 and 3->2).
Spawned at start; likely a timing/tick helper used to wake/resume the U8P_HOURGLAS::anim process periodically.
Emit the short description string "clock " to the caller/player.
Query the global time/phase service repeatedly to obtain a numeric code used to select a period name for the message.
Display the constructed time message (or broken-clock fallback) to the player/process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides event handlers for an hourglass item: an animation tick handler that advances/toggles visual frames and plays a sound on certain transitions, a look handler that describes the object, and a use handler that reports the current in-game time/phase to the player.
